channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What to know about the deadly shooting of UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: The masked gunman who killed the leader of one of the largest U.S. health insurance companies in front of midtown Manhattan surveillance cameras remained free Thursday from the dragnet thrown by the nation’s largest police department.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Ammunition used in CEO’s killing had 'Deny,' 'defend' and 'depose' written on it, AP source says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The masked gunman who stalked and killed the leader of one of the largest U.S. health insurance companies outside a Manhattan hotel used ammunition emblazoned with the words “deny,” “defend” and “depose,” a law enforcement official said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Illinois homeowner feels underground mine is splitting her house in two
item.description: About 330,000 Illinois homes sit on top of old abandoned mines. Some say their homes are sinking because of it.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, KREX 5

item.title: Russia's top military officer gave his US counterpart advance notice of Mediterranean Sea drills
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Russia's top military officer called his U.S. counterpart to give advance warning of Russia's drills in the Mediterranean Sea, officials said Thursday, in what was a rare contact amid high tensions between Moscow and Washington. Russia's General Staff chief, Gen.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO assassin left message behind to 'make a statement' or 'throw off police': Detectives
item.description: Words etched on bullet casings at the scene of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson's murder were left behind "to make a statement," an NYPD detective said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK leader Starmer vows to take on 'alliance of naysayers' as he outlines plan for change
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer vowed Thursday to take on the “alliance of naysayers” and the bureaucratic “nonsense” that have hampered construction projects in the U.K.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Syrian insurgents capture central city of Hama in another blow to Syrian president
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Syrian insurgents entered the central city of Hama Thursday after days of intense clashes with government forces as they push forward with their week-long offensive that brought large parts of Syria under their control.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Blinken, Russia's Lavrov clash on Ukraine at a security meeting in Malta
item.description: VALLETTA, Malta (AP) — Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov clashed Thursday indirectly with U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken at an annual security meeting, accusing the West of risking escalation over Ukraine but walking out before Blinken and other speakers could respond.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: NASA's stuck astronauts hit 6 months in space. Just 2 more to go
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — Known across the globe as the stuck astronauts, Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams hit the six-month mark in space Thursday with two more to go.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Waymo unveils plan to bring its robotaxi service to Miami
item.description: Waymo is gearing up to bring its robotaxi service to Miami which will accelerate an expansion that's been happening while its hobbled rivals remain in its rearview mirror.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Concerns mount over Musk’s taste for revenge
item.description: Concerns were amplified last week after Musk went after retired Army Lt. Col. Alexander Vindman, who became an outspoken critic of President-elect Trump after testifying in his first impeachment trial.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge considers first lawsuit to overturn Missouri's near-total abortion ban
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Abortion-rights advocates on Wednesday asked a judge to overturn Missouri’s near-total ban on the procedure, less than a month after voters backed an abortion-rights constitutional amendment. Jackson County Circuit Judge Jerri Zhang did not immediately rule after the hearing.
item.dc:creator: SUMMER BALLENTINE and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump picks Monica Crowley for senior State Department post
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday nominated Monica Crowley, a former Fox News contributor who faced public scrutiny during Trump’s first term over allegations of plagiarism, for a top public relations post in the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Is it hotter than the sun outside? Wacky weather app bug says it is
item.description: If you took a look at the weather widget on your Windows computer Wednesday afternoon, you may have noticed that the numbers were higher than normal for winter.
item.dc:creator: Joey Rather, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's picks for key positions in his second administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has filled the key posts for his second term in office, prioritizing loyalty to him after he felt bruised and hampered by internal squabbling during his first term.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump taps Gail Slater to lead DOJ antitrust division
item.description: Gail Slater will take over the DOJ's high-profile antitrust cases against tech giants like Google and Apple.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold Tennessee's ban on medical treatments for transgender minors
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Hearing a high-profile culture-war clash, the Supreme Court on Wednesday seemed likely to uphold Tennessee's ban on gender-affirming care for minors.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane Helene flood victims face new challenges as temperatures drop
item.description: "I know of over 100 people that are living in campers currently, and that's just my immediate service area," said volunteer Ashley Tomberg.
item.dc:creator: Brody Wilson, NewsNation

item.title: Union rights take center stage in already high-stakes Wisconsin Supreme Court race
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin's state Supreme Court election next spring already had high stakes, with majority control on the line.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump moves to toss Georgia criminal case as president-elect
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday asked to throw out his criminal election interference charges in Georgia following his election victory. Trump’s two federal indictments have already been tossed, and the former president is attempting to do the same in his New York hush money case.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio school to offer new religious release program through Satanic Temple
item.description: A new religious release program for students is entering public schools in Marysville, Ohio, and this one emphasizes Satanic studies.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, KRON4

item.title: Puppy food sold in 7 states recalled due to salmonella contamination: FDA
item.description: A litter of puppies became ill after eating the mix, a customer told the Virginia Department of Agriculture and Consumer Services.
item.dc:creator: Michael Reiner, Fox 5

item.title: Manhattan DA's downplaying of Daniel Penny's potential punishment 'improper and misleading': defense
item.description: What's behind Manhattan DA Alvin Bragg's push to downplay the potential consequences for Daniel Penny, if his office gets a conviction in the Jordan Neely case?
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trooper, the dog abandoned on I-75 before Hurricane Milton, finds forever home
item.description: Trooper, the dog who was rescued by the Florida Highway Patrol during Hurricane Milton has now been adopted, the Leon County Humane Society announced.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Stadium-size asteroid to make closest approach to Earth
item.description: The stadium-size asteroid is considered a "potentially hazardous object," but poses no threat to Earth this year.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Harris found success with women who have cats, but Trump got the dog owner vote: AP VoteCast
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The lead-up to the 2024 election was all about cat owners. But in the end, the dogs had their day.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, HUMERA LODHI and ANNIE NG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Spotify Wrapped: How to view, share your personalized recap
item.description: Want to show everyone who your top five artists were or just how much time you spent listening to music and podcasts? Now is your chance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Prosecutors: DC police officer’s talk with Proud Boys leader grew secretive as arrest neared
item.description: Metropolitan Police Department Lt. Shane Lamond’s communication with the former leader of the Proud Boys increased and grew more secretive as pressure to arrest him mounted in 2020, prosecutors said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: Train slams into stuck semi-truck in Pennsylvania
item.description: City police believe the truck got hung up on the snow and the driver couldn't pull away before having to exit the cab before the train struck.
item.dc:creator: Bill Palmer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California teacher dies after suspected bat bite in her classroom
item.description: A central California middle school teacher died after contracting rabies from a suspected bat bite, local health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: Biden’s pardon of son Hunter threatens to tarnish legacy
item.description: President Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter in his final full month in the White House is threatening to erode his legacy as he prepares to leave office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Zach Bryan fan strikes lottery gold during marathon concert road trip
item.description: A whirlwind road trip to see country music star Zach Bryan in concert ended on an even higher note for a California mother and her teen son, thanks to a winning lottery ticket.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: More than 100 unaccompanied kids found at US border: Texas official
item.description: According to Texas Department of Public Safety Lt. Chris Olivarez, the children they've encountered ranged from 2 years old to 17 years old.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Jamie Komoroski sentenced for Folly Beach DUI crash that killed new bride
item.description: CHARLESTON, S.C. (WCBD) – The woman accused of drunkenly crashing into a golf cart, killing a bride, and seriously injuring members of the wedding party on Folly Beach last year was sentenced during a Monday afternoon hearing in Charleston for charges related to the incident.
item.dc:creator: Jameson Moyer, Fox 5

item.title: Most fun cities in U.S. Ranked. Here's where Los Angeles landed
item.description: Do Angelenos have the most fun? Well, it might depend on your idea of a good time. A new study by Wallet Hub ranked Los Angeles 18th on its list of Most Fun Cities in America but the City of Angels did finish first in a couple of key metrics.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Chainsaw-wielding man killed by police at assisted living facility in Illinois
item.description: The man had gotten into the lobby of the building and started confronting residents with the chainsaw, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, Fox 5

item.title: Boulder Police address JonBenét Ramsey's case after Netflix docuseries premieres
item.description: Without specifically addressing the docuseries, Boulder Police called many of the claims in the show "completely false.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, WFLA, NBC
